message: Live happily!
Ludwig Josef Johann Wittgenstein   July 8, 1916
The end of summer.
Even at the end of summer, the sun shone high in the sky.
Cumulonimbi gathered strength far off in the distance.
It'd been a while since I came out to Hasaki's apartment.
The Sanami woman.
That's what they always called my mother in the village.
Her expression only barely changed when she saw me.

name: Kotomi
message: "T-Takuji?"
I shook my head.

name: Tomosane
message: "I'm Tomosane. Mamiya Tomosane."

name: Kotomi
message: "Tomo...sane..."

name: Tomosane
message: "That's right. Tomosane, the child you had with Hiroo. Takuji is dead."

name: Kotomi
message: "Takuji is... dead..."

name: Tomosane
message: "That's right. Don't you remember?"

name: Kotomi
message: "Takuji is... dead..."
She mumbled those words with an empty heart. Then she just turned back to the wall and stared, unmoving.

name: Tomosane
message: "Nothing has changed..."

name: Hasaki
message: "That's not true at all."

name: Tomosane
message: "Really?"

name: Hasaki
message: "Yeah, she started getting better after July 20th last year. It's a miracle that she can even hold a basic conversation like that."

name: Tomosane
message: "I see."

name: Hasaki
message: "But this is the most dangerous time for her. I have to keep a close eye on her."

name: Tomosane
message: "The most dangerous time?"

name: Hasaki
message: "When she's just talking to the wall, I don't have to worry about her committing suicide, but when she has enough willpower to talk to people, then she might also have enough willpower to do something."

name: Tomosane
message: "She has willpower... In other words, you think there's the possibility of her committing suicide?"

name: Hasaki
message: "That's right. I have to take her to the Mamiya house and watch over her."

name: Tomosane
message: "Hasaki."

name: Hasaki
message: "Hm? What?"

name: Tomosane
message: "You know, I... I hate this woman so much I could kill her. The blame for this entire tragedy lies on her shoulders."

name: Hasaki
message: "Yeah. You might be right."

name: Tomosane
message: "You should hate her even more than I do, so why are you taking care of her?"

name: Hasaki
message: "Because she's my mother."

name: Tomosane
message: "Even if that's what you believe, she threw you away."

name: Hasaki
message: "You just didn't know her, Tomo Nii-san."

name: Tomosane
message: "I didn't know her?"

name: Hasaki
message: "She... didn't love Dad, and that's why she committed that taboo and gave birth to us, right?"
Those words silenced me.
I didn't understand what train of thought could lead her to that conclusion.

name: Tomosane
message: "You know, Hasaki, you don't need to make excuses for her."

name: Hasaki
message: "No, it's the truth. It's not a question of perspective. It's the truth."

name: Tomosane
message: "The truth?"

name: Hasaki
message: "She loved Dad, and she loved Takuji Nii-san, you, and even me. She really loved us."

name: Tomosane
message: "That's ridiculous. That's not true!"

name: Hasaki
message: "Dad died very young, didn't he? He knew that he didn't have very much time left."

name: Tomosane
message: "He knew?"

name: Hasaki
message: "Yeah. He had a really bad, chronic condition. Especially after you went back to the village, he constantly needed dialysis or else he was at risk of dying."

name: Tomosane
message: "But he had cancer."

name: Hasaki
message: "Yeah, he did. He knew that either way, he wouldn't live for much longer."

name: Hasaki
message: "You probably didn't know, but I heard from the doctor at the hospital after he died."

name: Hasaki
message: "If not for his chronic condition, they could've healed his cancer without a problem."

name: Tomosane
message: "They could've?"

name: Hasaki
message: "Mom wanted to save him because he was the only person who protected her when she was persecuted by everyone else in the village."

name: Hasaki
message: "She just wanted to save Dad..."

name: Hasaki
message: "That's why she started experimenting with new religions. She was tricked by that new cult's pastor."

name: Tomosane
message: "The White Lotus Association..."

name: Hasaki
message: "Yeah, and Mom was a beautiful woman. More importantly, she was the successor to the bloodline of the Sanagi shrine maidens."

name: Hasaki
message: "In the religious industry, the traditional shrine maiden bloodline simply has market value."

name: Tomosane
message: "Market value?"

name: Hasaki
message: "Yeah, the pastor and the isolated woman... He wanted to have a child with the shrine maiden who had inherited her powers from ancient times."

name: Hasaki
message: "He didn't even know for sure if the Sanami family had real powers, but he wanted people to believe that they did."

name: Hasaki
message: "And as for the rest of the organization, don't you think they would support this child as the next pastor too?"

name: Tomosane
message: "Just because of her family name?"

name: Hasaki
message: "Yes. She only did it because she thought that Dad would be saved if she gave birth to the savior."

name: Hasaki
message: "That's not all. She thought that the pastor could help her make peace with the village people who persecuted her as well."

name: Tomosane
message: "Th-That's ridiculous..."

name: Hasaki
message: "It's true. She was desperate because she wanted to save Dad, and she regretted that he couldn't go back to the village because he eloped with her. She was trying to make peace with them."

name: Tomosane
message: "That's... That's too stupid..."

name: Hasaki
message: "She was a fool. In truth, she was naive. So naive that she slept with a man she didn't like and bore his child."

name: Hasaki
message: "All to birth a savior who would save the world."

name: Tomosane
message: "But for better or worse, she gave birth to twins."

name: Hasaki
message: "What I think is the pastor had grown tired of her, since he was only using her for her body."

name: Hasaki
message: "He had plenty of other women anyway. So when Mom gave birth to twins, he made up an excuse to get rid of her by saying that Takuji Nii-san had lost his qualification to be the savior."

name: Hasaki
message: "She was thrown away just because he got tired of using her."

name: Hasaki
message: "But she still tried to raise Takuji Nii-san as the savior. She believed that it was still possible."

name: Hasaki
message: "It's true that Mom and Takuji Nii-san were cold to me, but it was even harder on Mom. She was trying to accomplish something that no human could ever accomplish."

name: Hasaki
message: "That's why... the day that Dad died, Mom was completely broken by the shock."

name: Hasaki
message: "She gave birth to a child that would be the savior just so she could save Dad... but her plan was fruitless, and he died anyway."

name: Tomosane
message: "Then why did she try to hurt you?"

name: Hasaki
message: "She seriously believed that Takuji Nii-san could cross the border between life and death if he awakened to his powers as the savior."

name: Hasaki
message: "She believed that if she could just kill me and restore the powers that Takuji Nii-san originally had, then he would be able to bring me back to life afterward, and his father as well."

name: Tomosane
message: "What do you mean?"

name: Hasaki
message: "If I died and Takuji Nii-san's powers came back, Dad and I would both be able to come back to life."

name: Tomosane
message: "That's totally absurd."

name: Hasaki
message: "Yeah... It's absurd."

name: Hasaki
message: "But, you know, I can understand it."

name: Hasaki
message: "You heard she was bullied in that village, right?"

name: Hasaki
message: "I wasn't bullied like that, but it really felt like I didn't even exist, both at home and at school. It's not like I can't understand how she must've felt."

name: Hasaki
message: "I always thought I was just an unnecessary person. I was just someone who didn't matter much either way. I wonder if that's what she thought to herself too."

name: Hasaki
message: "And then for the first time in your life, someone in this world discovers that you exist."

name: Hasaki
message: "You would do absolutely anything for that person."

name: Hasaki
message: "If you've always felt like you weren't needed, then if there was anything in the world you could do for the one person who finally loved you, you would do it."

name: Hasaki
message: "Because that's how I feel, too."

name: Tomosane
message: "Hasaki..."

name: Hasaki
message: "I would do anything to save you, Tomo Nii-san."

name: Hasaki
message: "I would die if it meant that you could live. If I had to sacrifice my body in order to protect you, then I would do it without hesitation."

name: Hasaki
message: "Maybe that's because I got that from her. We really are mother and daughter."

name: Hasaki
message: "But my heart is weak, so if there wasn't anyone who needed my sullied body anymore, then I would just die, right then and there."

name: Hasaki
message: "But Mom wanted to save him with all her heart. That was the only reason why she betrayed him."

name: Hasaki
message: "Hahahaha... Mom really was a fool."

name: Tomosane
message: "Hasaki..."
I was at a loss for words.
Until this very moment, I'd lived my entire life hating my mother.
Now I didn't have it in me to truly hate this woman.
She was just a fool, and her foolish attempts to find happiness caused all this.
Even then, I just think she was...

name: Tomosane
message: "She was so stupid. She really was a fool."

name: Hasaki
message: "Yeah, and so was her daughter, me..."

name: Tomosane
message: "Yeah..."
Even the person I had pinned as the first cause in this entire tragedy... It's not that easy to just say that she was the root of all evil.
Of course, she wasn't without sin either.
Her sin was that she was ignorant, incompetent, and overly confident.
She was a fool, but she tried as hard as she could despite being a fool, and this was the result.
But I wonder if I can blame her alone.
I wonder if my father's choice was right.
I wonder if he made the wrong choice.
What about Hasaki's choice?
What about mine?
Or Takuji's decision, which led to his death.
And then Yuki-nee's decision...
I wonder if their decisions were right. How much faith did they have in their own decisions?
Each one believed their decision was the right one, and this was the result.
People like to attach a single cause to the problems they face.
But the cause of a tragedy like this can't be traced back to one decision.
The combination of several correct individual choices can even cause terrible tragedy.
In that sense...
It must be true that the road to hell is paved with good intentions.
Man is blissfully unaware as he walks along that road to hell.

name: Hasaki
message: "What's wrong, Tomo Nii-san?"

name: Tomosane
message: "Oh, nothing. It's nothing."
My mother just looked at the wall, same as ever.
What did she see there?
For a moment, I lost sight of the fundamental difference between her actions and the actions carried out by all the rest of humanity over the course of their lives.
I quoted the words of a philosopher.
The words of the world's most difficult philosopher.

name: Tomosane
message: "The subject does not belong to the world but it is a limit of the world."

name: Tomosane
message: "The sense of the world must lie outside the world. In the world everything is as it is and happens as it does happen."

name: Tomosane
message: "Therefore, no value exists in the world."
No value exists in the world.
Not money,
or fame,
or women,
or dreams.
Not human rights,
or democracy,
or missiles,
or government.
Or religion,
or god,
or faith,
or thought.
Not philosophy,
or science,
or family,
or love.
And of course, every story ever told is just another part of the world.
All of them are neither outside the world nor the world's limits.

The world...
&
To put it in words, it's a container.
The world can't be anything other than a container.
A container can't be filled with other containers.
All the different materials which make up the world are incapable of filling us.
That's why I say this.
No one can tell you what you should fill that container with.

name: Hasaki
message: "What was that?"

name: Tomosane
message: "Huh? I was just talking to myself. Forget it."

name: Hasaki
message: "Grr."

name: Tomosane
message: "Wh-What?"

name: Hasaki
message: "You and Yuki-san are always saying things that are hard to understand, like you figured something out. It's not fair."

name: Tomosane
message: "What do you mean, it's not fair? Besides, it's not a big deal."

name: Hasaki
message: "But it sounded like it was really hard to understand."

name: Tomosane
message: "Hard to understand? It wasn't anything important."

name: Tomosane
message: "It's something that everyone knows. The old man down the street, the cashier at the dollar store, and even the bearded taxi driver."

name: Hasaki
message: "They all understand what you just said?"

name: Tomosane
message: "Yeah."

name: Hasaki
message: "Wh-Why is that?"

name: Tomosane
message: "Live happily!"

name: Tomosane
message: "That's all."

name: Hasaki
message: "Jeez, I don't get it."

name: Tomosane
message: "Don't overthink it. The most important thing to remember is that it's a command."

name: Hasaki
message: "A command?"

name: Tomosane
message: "Yes. Those who consider themselves unfortunate spend most of their time complaining. No matter how unfortunate you believe your life to be, you must live happily!"

name: Tomosane
message: "That's all."

name: Hasaki
message: "Whoa!"
I grabbed Hasaki's head in a vice grip.

name: Tomosane
message: "That's how you're already living, so don't worry about it."
I ruminated on the words of a genius, the world's most difficult philosopher.
Even though it's such an easy answer, which everyone already knows, arriving at that answer was difficult beyond measure.
The reason why is because God lives in these words.
"To believe in a God means to understand the question about the meaning of life."
"To believe in a God means to see that the facts of the world are not the end of the matter."
"To believe in a God means to see that life has a meaning."
That God doesn't need to work miracles.
He didn't create the world in one week.
He fundamentally does nothing.
Even then, he irresponsibly tells us...
He whispers in our ears that we must "live happily."
All he did was establish the workings of the universe, and he didn't do it for anyone's sake in particular.
That's the truth of the person we call God.
All the world's lies, injustices, fakes, lechers, and filth... All of those are permitted by God.
No matter what injustices befall us in life, God still says to us.
"Live happily!"
On a whim, I decided to say something to my mother.

name: Tomosane
message: "Live happily. That's what God has told us."
She looked at me with dead eyes.
So I said to her...

name: Tomosane
message: "Everything has been saved. Hasaki and me were saved, and so were you, Takuji, and Dad, too."

name: Tomosane
message: "Don't drown in your sorrow. That sorrow is nothing more than a liquor to get you drunk on happiness."

name: Tomosane
message: "You shouldn't use alcohol to fill the void inside you, though."

name: Tomosane
message: "What container is worth filling?"
I posed that question to my mother.
I was just talking to myself.
I left the apartment.

name: Hasaki
message: "I'm going to the store."

name: Tomosane
message: "Yeah, sure. I'm gonna go home and take a nap."

name: Hasaki
message: "What are you talking about? You have a shift to work tonight."

name: Tomosane
message: "Do I?"

name: Hasaki
message: "You do!
And you can't skip out on work today."

name: Tomosane
message: "Yeah, I gotcha."
Hasaki and I parted ways after that.
Hasaki went straight to the train station, but I decided to walk.
I walked through the unfamiliar city and came to the river.
If you follow this road, you'll come to a place near Suginomiya.
I walked alone down the river terrace.
"Hey, Unidentified Minor #1! It's been a while!"
I met another person I didn't really want to see.
Or in his case, the probability is higher that I was tailed by a person I didn't really want to see.
"So the trial is over... Congratulations."

name: Kimura
message: "Even if you were found not guilty, is it really safe for you to be out walking alone?"

name: Kimura
message: "Your face and name got posted all over the Internet. There were a lot of people who weren't happy with that ruling."

name: Tomosane
message: "Seems like it, huh?"

name: Kimura
message: "Yeah, for sure. Is it okay for you to be taking a leisurely walk outside?"

name: Tomosane
message: "It's been more than a year since that incident. Nothing has happened so far, so it should be fine, right?"

name: Kimura
message: "No, no, don't you think you could get in trouble?"

name: Tomosane
message: "How? I haven't had any trouble. I'm just living my life like normal."

name: Kimura
message: "Really? Isn't it scary that a bunch of people you don't know are talking about you?"

name: Tomosane
message: "I'm not afraid of what other people think. They're not people I know."

name: Kimura
message: "Oh, look at you. You really are strong."

name: Tomosane
message: "I don't care if they're not happy about the ruling. If they want to do something, then they should confront me directly."

name: Kimura
message: "Directly? You know that there's no one who can fight you and hope to win.

name: Tomosane
message: "A few people tried, though."

name: Kimura
message: "They did?"

name: Tomosane
message: "They were gangsters or thugs, I think. I'm not sure, but they thought they were tough. They came after me a few times."

name: Kimura
message: "Ahaha... I see. How did that go?"

name: Tomosane
message: "Nothing really happened. They weren't any different from the guys I ran into around here. None of them were on the same level as Yuki or Master."

name: Kimura
message: "It seems like a lot of lowlifes do that nowadays. Some of them even post on social media that they're looking for someone to fight."

name: Kimura
message: "Your picture and address might've been posted on those sites, you know?"

name: Tomosane
message: "I don't really care."

name: Kimura
message: "Jeez, you're always so cool-headed. You could at least humor me and look a little upset."

name: Tomosane
message: "Are you still investigating?"

name: Kimura
message: "If I called it an investigation... it would be a lie."

name: Kimura
message: "You're not valuable enough for a weekly magazine to keep chasing now."

name: Tomosane
message: "Then why are you here?"

name: Kimura
message: "Just personal interest. Is that good enough?"

name: Tomosane
message: "Hmph. Do you think I'm going to play along?"

name: Kimura
message: "Who knows?"
Kimura slowly followed after me when I walked away.
I had no reason to run away either, so we kept walking, looking as if we were going somewhere together.

name: Kimura
message: "What was the deal with that whole incident, anyway?"

name: Kimura
message: "Why did it turn into such a ridiculous situation?"

name: Tomosane
message: "It's your job to figure that out."

name: Kimura
message: "Yeah, it's my job."

name: Tomosane
message: "But didn't you already finish that job?"

name: Kimura
message: "Yeah, but my hobby is just an extension of my job, so I don't really mind."

name: Tomosane
message: "How convenient."
I wanted to point out all the flaws in his logic, but...

name: Kimura
message: "I was thinking... I wonder why so many people played along with his absurd tragedy."

name: Kimura
message: "Normally, no one would play along with that kind of absurd farce."

name: Kimura
message: "How did he even know the world was ending? And why would someone want to kill themselves before it ends? I don't understand it."

name: Tomosane
message: "He didn't want anyone to kill themselves. From his perspective, they were all just returning to the sky."

name: Kimura
message: "Now that you mention it, there were a lot of people who took part in that group suicide even though they weren't students at Kita High."

name: Kimura
message: "Even those who weren't alumni at the school―people who were completely unrelated―were involved."

name: Kimura
message: "Group hysteria... That's what they called it in the newspapers and magazines."

name: Kimura
message: "But then why did so many unrelated people end up dying?"

name: Kimura
message: "That kind of hysteria only occurs in group settings. People from all over, even completely unrelated people, gathered in that place."

name: Tomosane
message: "I don't know much about this, but aren't they saying that all that crazy stuff happened because the whole group was suffering from hysteria?"

name: Kimura
message: "I guess that's what they've been saying, but that doesn't really explain anything, does it?"

name: Kimura
message: "None of those unrelated people were registered on the underground message board either."

name: Kimura
message: "I don't know how they all heard about it, but it seems like they all came for different reasons."

name: Tomosane
message: "Apparently some people just heard rumors that they could have sex there."

name: Kimura
message: "Yeah, of course, but isn't it ridiculous that they would die just to have sex?"

name: Tomosane
message: "I guess so."

name: Kimura
message: "But after I spent so much time reading the forums, I'm not really surprised that people would do that."

name: Tomosane
message: "They would die just to have sex?"

name: Kimura
message: "I guess they would. It seems ridiculous to me."

name: Kimura
message: "It actually seems like a pretty common scene in this world... That's what I think."

name: Tomosane
message: "A pretty common scene?"

name: Kimura
message: "Yeah, it can even seem perfectly natural for this kind of incident to happen. Sometimes I fall prey to that illusion."

name: Tomosane
message: "No, I don't think it can be natural."

name: Kimura
message: "Of course, it doesn't actually happen that easily, but sometimes it really does happen."

name: Tomosane
message: "Sometimes?"

name: Kimura
message: "Every time we hear rumors about missile attacks from neighboring countries, a new strain of a virus that could wipe out humanity, or a prophecy about some great disaster that is going to strike us."

name: Kimura
message: "It happens every time."

name: Tomosane
message: "What does?"

name: Kimura
message: "They say, 'Here it is at last.'"

name: Tomosane
message: "Here it is at last?"

name: Kimura
message: "A young man once said that war is the only solution, and he became famous overnight."

name: Kimura
message: "If a war starts, then it will at least change the situation we're stuck in. So sometimes we need to wage war."

name: Kimura
message: "In a sense, that's a logical way of thinking."

name: Kimura
message: "But that's not all it is."

name: Kimura
message: "Every time someone predicts a great disaster, you hear these words."
Now I can finally die.
Now it's finally over.

name: Tomosane
message: "Hmm... If you want to die, then you should just go and die already."

name: Kimura
message: "No, I totally agree. They should."

name: Kimura
message: "If they want to die, then they should. Why do they have to wait for some disaster to kill them?"

name: Kimura
message: "That's right..."

name: Kimura
message: "But you know, every time one of these disasters is predicted, you see people online saying that they can finally die."

name: Kimura
message: "Even if it's not quite the end of the world, all they need is a large-scale disaster."

name: Kimura
message: "With that, they can say that it's finally over."

name: Kimura
message: "Why do they look for a reason to end it?"

name: Tomosane
message: "A reason..."

name: Kimura
message: "Yes... A reason to die."

name: Tomosane
message: "I see. So then what are you trying to say, as a journalist?"

name: Tomosane
message: "Are you trying to say that our repressive society caused this incident?"

name: Kimura
message: "Oh, that's a good one. Our repressive society... The feeling of repression spreading among our youth is the real cause of this incident! That would be a good headline."

name: Kimura
message: "Hahahaha, you have talent, Tomosane-kun. Why don't you become a writer? You wouldn't make it as a journalist, but we still have plenty of room for extra writers."

name: Tomosane
message: "Haa... What did you really come here for?"

name: Kimura
message: "Hahahaha... Our repressive society, huh?
This country has definitely been dominated by that kind of atmosphere for a while now."

name: Kimura
message: "This city, you know..."

name: Kimura
message: "Once upon a time, people called this place a new town."

name: Kimura
message: "That was before I was born. It was a long time ago that this city was new."

name: Kimura
message: "It was new before we ever knew it."

name: Kimura
message: "All sorts of things were new before we were ever born."

name: Kimura
message: "A world of only new things..."

name: Kimura
message: "Everything was new, and everything in the world was complete."

name: Tomosane
message: "Haa... Can I say something?"

name: Tomosane
message: "Don't you think there were a lot of people who felt like they were being repressed, even in the distant past?"

name: Tomosane
message: "It's not just the modern generation. There have always been those people."

name: Kimura
message: "Oh, then how about this?"

name: Kimura
message: "How vast is the earth? How far is the past? I face a great foe, and hope I will last."

name: Kimura
message: "Learnt of Horatio, but truly all for naught."

name: Kimura
message: "The world's lies, all told..."

name: Kimura
message: "The truth which I sought."

name: Kimura
message: "Fire burns in my chest. I seek my final rest."

name: Kimura
message: "No hell for fearing, nor lord for saving. Now I see with hope, hopelessness melding."

name: Tomosane
message: "Is that Fujimura Misao?"

name: Kimura
message: "Oh, I thought you would recognize it. That was a poem called 'To Stand Upon a Great Precipice.'"

name: Tomosane
message: "I don't really understand, but that was the suicide note left by a high school student who committed suicide at the Kegon waterfall in the Meiji era, am I right?"

name: Kimura
message: "Yeah, yeah."

name: Tomosane
message: "I heard there were a lot of copycat suicides afterward, too."

name: Kimura
message: "That's true, there were. People always feel like they're being repressed by society, no matter what era it is."

name: Kimura
message: "As if their human life has no value in this universe, in this world..."

name: Kimura
message: "Not only in this era."

name: Tomosane
message: "Then..."

name: Kimura
message: "Then... what do you think?"

name: Kimura
message: "It's not something that only happens in the modern era. This wasn't the only time..."

name: Kimura
message: "But the only era we can live in is this one."

name: Kimura
message: "We aren't living in the same era as Fujimura Misao, Natsume Souseki, or Ozaki Housai."

name: Kimura
message: "The suffering we experience is our own, present suffering."

name: Kimura
message: "Not the suffering of someone from a distant past."

name: Kimura
message: "The pain we feel right now will never belong to anyone else. That's why it's precious."

name: Tomosane
message: "Are you okay, Kimura-san? Did Mamiya Takuji decide to possess you next?"

name: Kimura
message: "No, no, hahahaha... No, I just got a bit worked up."

name: Tomosane
message: "I see. The pain we feel right now will never belong to anyone else, huh?"

name: Tomosane
message: "Well... Honestly, I think that's a pretty boring argument."

name: Kimura
message: "Really?"

name: Tomosane
message: "For instance, you say 'our' pain, when really it's just one person's pain. Your pronoun usage is all over the place."

name: Kimura
message: "Oh wow, I guess you're right."

name: Tomosane
message: "But I understand that feeling."

name: Tomosane
message: "The feeling when you want to say, 'This is my pain, not anyone else's.'"
Kimura and I kept walking on and on along the river embankment.
On and on...

name: Kimura
message: "What I'm trying to get at is... what is our raison d'etre?"

name: Tomosane
message: "Raison d'etre?"

name: Kimura
message: "I mean to ask what our life is worth."

name: Kimura
message: "The meaning of life..."

name: Kimura
message: "Well, everyone gets a bit existential when they're teenagers. It's an unsolvable problem that everyone thinks about, and everyone believes a certain way about, but no one can answer for sure."

name: Kimura
message: "Maybe that's why the question has such an allure."

name: Tomosane
message: "Do you think so?"

name: Kimura
message: "Don't you?"

name: Kimura
message: "Well, it certainly depends on the person, too. Some people worry about it more than others."

name: Kimura
message: "But everyone who's ever felt powerless has worried about the meaning of their life, right?"

name: Kimura
message: "Is there ultimately a meaning to my life, or yours, or anyone else's? To any human life? Is there some meaning behind our existence? Everyone wonders that sometimes."

name: Tomosane
message: "I see."

name: Kimura
message: "That's the question you have to wrestle with all your life, no matter how rich or poor you are.
Well, it's still better to have money, though."

name: Kimura
message: "People always feel a certain anxiety toward their own raison d'etre. That question of why we exist."

name: Kimura
message: "D'ou venons-nous? Que Sommes-nous? Ou allons-nous?"

name: Tomosane
message: "Where do we come from? What are we? Where are we going?"

name: Kimura
message: "That's right. That was the final painting by the French artist Paul Gauguin. He gave it that name right before he committed suicide."

name: Tomosane
message: "It was technically just a suicide attempt, though."

name: Kimura
message: "Hahahaha, that's right."

name: Kimura
message: "The moon which is a dream, and the sixpence which is reality... Where did we come from, and where we going?"

name: Tomosane
message: "Isn't it all just from the cradle to the grave?"

name: Kimura
message: "Hahahaha, what's that? You mean to say that the object of human effort should always be on improving our lives in this world?"

name: Tomosane
message: "That's what a human life is, isn't it?"

name: Kimura
message: "It might be..."

name: Kimura
message: "That might be all there is in our lives."

name: Kimura
message: "Especially for us, who were born into a world where everything has already been set up and manufactured for us. Isn't it kind of cruel?"

name: Tomosane
message: "Hehehe... Now we're criticizing modern society?"

name: Kimura
message: "Ahahaha, I guess so. I suppose that's what it sounds like, ahaha."
How long had we been walking?
The water flowing in the river now reflected a sheen of golden light.
Our two shadows, standing side by side, had also grown longer and darker.

name: Tomosane
message: "Is there any meaning to the pieces of a jigsaw puzzle?"

name: Kimura
message: "Huh? What do you mean?"

name: Tomosane
message: "Every individual piece of a jigsaw puzzle has no meaning whatsoever."

name: Tomosane
message: "Each one is completely meaningless in its existence."

name: Tomosane
message: "From the way you're talking, a human being is an existence no better than a single piece of a jigsaw puzzle."

name: Kimura
message: "No better than a single piece of a jigsaw puzzle..."

name: Tomosane
message: "Yes. Without a place to fit. For instance, if a person has no place they belong, then that one piece has no meaning."

name: Tomosane
message: "One piece of a jigsaw puzzle. One misshapen fragment."

name: Tomosane
message: "Without a place where it fits, it's just an ugly, meaningless existence."

name: Kimura
message: "That's..."

name: Tomosane
message: "But is a human life really equal to a single puzzle piece?"

name: Tomosane
message: "Are we really puzzle pieces?"

name: Tomosane
message: "A piece of a puzzle has a place where it fits because of its outside edge."

name: Kimura
message: "Because of its outside edge?"

name: Tomosane
message: "Yeah. It fits right into a place in the puzzle because of its exterior."

name: Kimura
message: "Its exterior..."

name: Tomosane
message: "Yes, its exterior."

name: Tomosane
message: "Take that argument to its logical end. Where is the outside edge on my world, or the outside edge on your world?"

name: Kimura
message: "The outside edge of my world..."

name: Tomosane
message: "That's right. Where is the world which your world is supposed to fit into?"

name: Tomosane
message: "The place where my world fits perfectly like a piece of a jigsaw puzzle... Does such a larger world even exist?"

name: Tomosane
message: "What I think is..."

name: Tomosane
message: "We have no exterior."

name: Tomosane
message: "Our world has no exterior."

name: Tomosane
message: "This world, including you, this river, the sun, and even the bloodshot sky overhead."

name: Tomosane
message: "There is no exterior or anything."

name: Tomosane
message: "It's all just the world."

name: Tomosane
message: "It's all just my world."

name: Kimura
message: "Is that solipsism?"

name: Kimura
message: "The world is only in your head. Your own existence is the world."

name: Tomosane
message: "Who knows? I'm not sure, myself."

name: Tomosane
message: "I don't really feel like I'm alone in the world."

name: Tomosane
message: "You're right in front of my eyes, so you definitely exist. I would even go so far as to say that Minakami Yuki and the Wakatsuki sisters existed as well, even if they didn't exist to you."

name: Tomosane
message: "But still, even then, my world ends at the limit of my world."

name: Tomosane
message: "I don't know anything beyond the limit of my own world. I have no way of knowing anything else."

name: Tomosane
message: "That's why I'm only me."

name: Tomosane
message: "It's a bit strange for me to say this after sharing my body with multiple people. No, especially because of that, I can say that I'm no one except myself."

name: Tomosane
message: "I'm not this arm, this leg, this heart, this body, or even this brain."

name: Tomosane
message: "Of course, I'm not the road, the river, or the sky either."

name: Tomosane
message: "I'm me."

name: Tomosane
message: "And my world is the world. There is no exterior to that world."

name: Tomosane
message: "So I don't need a meaning."

name: Tomosane
message: "My world needs no addendum."

name: Tomosane
message: "The world is not a piece of a jigsaw puzzle."

name: Tomosane
message: "Just look at how wide our world is. It extends out into the infinite vastness of space and time."

name: Kimura
message: "Space and time?"

name: Tomosane
message: "How wide is our brain?"

name: Tomosane
message: "The brain is wider than the sky."

name: Tomosane
message: "For, put them side by side, the one the other will include..."

name: Tomosane
message: "With ease, and you beside."

name: Tomosane
message: "The brain is deeper than the sea."

name: Tomosane
message: "For, hold them, blue to blue..."

name: Tomosane
message: "The one the other will absorb..."

name: Tomosane
message: "As sponges, buckets do."

name: Tomosane
message: "The brain is just the weight of God."

name: Tomosane
message: "For, lift them, pound for pound..."

name: Tomosane
message: "And they will differ, if they do..."

name: Tomosane
message: "As syllable from sound."

name: Tomosane
message: "You know?"

name: Kimura
message: "What was that?"

name: Tomosane
message: "That was Emily Dickinson."

name: Kimura
message: "Oh, Dickinson, the American poet. It's impressive that you know her at your age."

name: Tomosane
message: "There's no need to look for a meaning in life."

name: Tomosane
message: "There's no need to hesitate in the face of an incomprehensible existence."

name: Tomosane
message: "There's no need to hesitate in the face of the incomprehensibility of this world, this universe, this sky, this river, or this road."

name: Tomosane
message: "It's all so vast that it can absorb everything which is a human life."

name: Tomosane
message: "It's just the size of God."

name: Tomosane
message: "It's just the weight of God."

name: Tomosane
message: "It's a beautiful syllable and a beautiful sound."
Kimura laughed.
His face kind of got on my nerves.

name: Kimura
message: "Ooh, you're pretty good too. You really know how to talk."

name: Tomosane
message: "Wha―"

name: Kimura
message: "Hehehehe... What's wrong? You always act so tough, like no one could ever get under your skin."

name: Tomosane
message: "I-I'm not trying to act tough or anything..."

name: Kimura
message: "Come on, it's nothing to get embarrassed about."

name: Tomosane
message: "Sh-Shut up!"

name: Kimura
message: "All right, but that was really funny."

name: Kimura
message: "We have no exterior..."

name: Kimura
message: "I see..."

name: Kimura
message: "It's true. Even if we had an exterior, it would just be another part of the world we know."

name: Kimura
message: "Maybe, at some point, we started looking for some kind of exterior. We started trying to see some kind of scene which we can never see."

name: Tomosane
message: "A scene that doesn't exist..."

name: Kimura
message: "That's right."

name: Kimura
message: "The far shore. A better place. An exterior world. The world of the gods. That's what it is, no matter what you call it."

name: Kimura
message: "So people fall into despair."

name: Kimura
message: "They despair of the meaning in their lives, the meaning in their worlds, the meaning in their selves."

name: Kimura
message: "Despair might be a word which describes people who are trying to see a scene which can never be seen."

name: Tomosane
message: "Trying to see a scene which can never be seen..."

name: Kimura
message: "Of course! When we try to speak of a scene which can never be seen, our words trip on themselves, standing meaningless in space and explaining nothing."

name: Kimura
message: "If you think of it that way, looking at the scene which can never be seen and feeling the emotion of despair, it's like unrequited love."

name: Tomosane
message: "Huh? Despair is unrequited love? What are you talking about?"

name: Kimura
message: "Why do people try to see the exterior which can never exist?"

name: Kimura
message: "Because they wish that it could exist!"

name: Kimura
message: "People look to the outside because they hope for it to exist. There might be no one out there, no one and nothing. They look to the outside because they're not even sure if that outside exists."

name: Kimura
message: "Why is that?"

name: Tomosane
message: "Haa... Because of that longing?"

name: Kimura
message: "That's right. Despair is longing."

name: Kimura
message: "And that longing is actually very similar to unrequited love."

name: Tomosane
message: "I don't agree at all."

name: Kimura
message: "Really? I think you agree, more than you know."

name: Tomosane
message: "Despair is longing, and that longing is like an unrequited love..."

name: Tomosane
message: "It's like hearing some desperate virgin who lashes out at people because of his insecurities."

name: Kimura
message: "Hm?"

name: Tomosane
message: "Huh?"

name: Kimura
message: "Hmmm?"

name: Tomosane
message: "Wh-What?"

name: Kimura
message: "Huuuh?"

name: Tomosane
message: "Wh-What are you laughing at?"

name: Kimura
message: "Yuuki-kun, weren't you a virgin?"

name: Tomosane
message: "Wha―Th-That's not..."

name: Kimura
message: "Hehehehe... You know, I might look dim, but I've investigated your past very thoroughly."

name: Kimura
message: "There were an awful lot of bad rumors about you, but in reality you don't have any experience with women."

name: Tomosane
message: "Wh-What have you been investigating?!"

name: Kimura
message: "Huh? That's my job, you know!"

name: Tomosane
message: "Th-Then maybe I am a virgin..."

name: Kimura
message: "Hehehehe... You guys can't legally get married, either."

name: Tomosane
message: "Wha―!"

name: Kimura
message: "There are ethical problems there, too."

name: Tomosane
message: "K-Kimura! Have you been going behind my back?!"

name: Kimura
message: "Hahahaha, but the law has nothing to do with real love. People will fall in love no matter what the punishment."

name: Kimura
message: "Romeo and Juliet!"

name: Tomosane
message: "No, that's not actually a story about incest..."

name: Kimura
message: "Oh? So it really is your sister?"

name: Tomosane
message: "Huh?!"

name: Kimura
message: "Ooh, I better get going!"

name: Tomosane
message: "Kimura, you asshole! Get back here!"

name: Kimura
message: "No thanks. I'd like to keep all my bones intact, if you don't mind."

name: Kimura
message: "Make sure you wear a rubber, kid. Not like I really care, though."

name: Tomosane
message: "Kimura!"
Kimura ran and jumped down off the embankment.
Then he disappeared into the city.
Dusk enveloped the city.
The sky slowly turned a darker shade of red, and the buildings' shadows grew longer by the moment.
When the world sank into darkness, the lights came on in the city.
The lights of human activity buzzed into life.

name: Tomosane
message: "Better get going. I have work tonight."
I had a shift to work tonight.
I play the piano at that same bar.
I play the piano however I feel like, and occasionally I even take requests from the customers. But mostly, I just play.
